principles: Creative and intellectual labor must remain visibly connected to its contributors.
Hosting, teaching, curation, critique, safety review, compute, data, code, art, and creative direction are distinct contribution classes and must not be collapsed into a single ownership claim.
Attribution does not automatically confer ownership, exclusivity, control, or future revenue rights.
Benefit-sharing terms must be explicit, consented to, versioned, and separable from attribution itself.
A contributor may choose public credit, pseudonymous credit, private escrowed credit, or no public credit while preserving an auditable private attestation.
No QSO, Sprite, host, platform, or fabric may erase prior valid attribution entries.
Corrections must append a superseding entry rather than rewrite history.
Derivative artifacts must identify upstream state certificates and material source contributions.
Heuristic influence scores must be labeled as estimates and must never be represented as legal ownership percentages.
Human reviewers retain final authority over disputed attribution, licensing, royalties, and benefit-sharing terms.
amendment_rule: A consuming QSO cannot amend this protocol. Changes require a new protocol version, explicit migration, and human review.
